danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite scaling and rendering in first-person pseudo-3D view; enemies approach from horizon
danger_signals.meaning: Combat encounter imminent; player must prepare or flee

danger_signals.visual_form: Side-scrolling view transition from overworld; darker color palette in detailed areas
danger_signals.meaning: High-danger zone; increased enemy density and environmental hazards

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual barriers rendered in pseudo-3D space; mountain silhouettes and rocky outcrops
danger_signals.meaning: Navigation obstacles; marks dangerous or restricted areas of continent

safety_signals.visual_form: Side-scrolling detailed view; NPCs present; interior/settlement architecture visible
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; opportunities for dialogue, trade, and quest information

safety_signals.visual_form: Top and bottom screen panels remain constant during overworld navigation; clear HUD visibility
safety_signals.meaning: Stable world state; player has agency and information accessibility

safety_signals.visual_form: Flat, accessible landscape in pseudo-3D view; minimal sprite congestion
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage available; low immediate threat

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: First-person perspective centering; protagonist controls navigation through overworld

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct sprite designs; appear at varying scales depending on proximity in pseudo-3D space
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Concentrated in dungeon areas and certain overworld regions; indicate territorial control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: First-person pseudo-3D engine; terrain shading and sprite scaling to simulate depth; mountainous boundaries enclosing navigable areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Free movement and exploration; encounters triggered by sprite proximity; undirected player agency emphasized

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to side-scrolling detailed 2D view; interior architecture and NPC placement; higher visual fidelity in localized areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Structured exploration; dialogue and interaction systems active; combat in dungeon subsections

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sprite scaling indicates enemy proximity; action RPG hybrid gameplay triggers; visual feedback through hit/damage indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Real-time beat 'em up mechanics activate; player dodge/attack; enemy AI responds to player position

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Well-translated first-person text overlay; story exposition through dialogue boxes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player-driven discovery; text directs attention to quest objectives and world lore; NPC interactions reveal political context

environmental_storytelling.example: Mountainlike enclosures partition the continent into distinct regions; visual geography reinforces exploration progression and regional identity
Town design, dungeon layout, and interior aesthetics communicate safety/danger zones; settlement density indicates civilization centers
High sprite congestion in certain regions signals danger zones; sparse enemy presence in others indicates neutral/safe territories
Transition from first-person pseudo-3D overworld to side-scrolling detailed view marks entry into narrative/action-focused spaces (towns, dungeons)
Basic terrain shading in overworld creates visual depth; potential darker tones in dungeons communicate increased threat

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Small sprite size on horizon; slow approach velocity; low threat
Scaling up in pseudo-3D space; sprite size increases; proximity triggers combat
Real-time movement and attack patterns; responsive to player actions; side-scrolling view active
Static placement; dialogue prompt available; signals interaction opportunity
Centered first-person view; controls sprite scaling and terrain interaction; agency-driven movement

notes: Dragon View uses a distinctive pseudo-3D overworld system (without dedicated 3D hardware like Super FX) that communicates world-state through sprite scaling and rudimentary rendering. The game's dual-perspective design—first-person overworld vs. side-scrolling detail views—creates clear visual demarcation between exploration and action/narrative spaces. Political/faction information appears to be conveyed primarily through first-person narrative text and NPC dialogue rather than visual iconography. The emphasis on player-driven undirected exploration means world-state is communicated reactively through visual consequence rather than prescriptive UI. Framerates suffer with high sprite density, which may serve as emergent danger signal. Limited source material prevents detailed faction color/symbol extraction.
